content_id: Dalil untuk shalat (as-shalat) dan zakat, dan penjelasan tentang tauhid adalah firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi: 'Padahal mereka tidak disuruh kecuali supaya menyembah Allah dengan memurnikan ketaatan kepada-Nya dalam (menjalankan) agama yang lurus, dan supaya mereka mendirikan shalat dan menunaikan zakat.' Artinya dalil bahwa shalat dan zakat adalah bagian dari agama adalah firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi: 'Padahal mereka tidak disuruh kecuali supaya menyembah Allah dengan memurnikan ketaatan kepada-Nya dalam (menjalankan) agama yang lurus, dan supaya mereka mendirikan shalat dan menunaikan zakat.' [Surat al-Bayyinah (98): 5]. Ayah ini bersifat umum dan mencakup semua jenis ibadah, sehingga seseorang harus melaksanakan semuanya dengan murni dan ikhlas untuk Allah Yang Maha Perkasa dan Agung, berada di atas agama yang lurus dan mengikuti jalan yang disyariatkan-Nya. Ini adalah contoh dari sesuatu yang khusus yang disebutkan setelah yang umum. Sehingga mendirikan shalat dan membayar zakat adalah jenis-jenis ibadah; namun Allah Yang Maha Suci dari segala kekurangan menyebutkannya secara khusus karena pentingnya yang besar. Sehingga shalat adalah ibadah badaniah, dan zakat adalah ibadah yang melibatkan pemberian harta, dan keduanya disebutkan bersama dalam Kitab Allah Yang Maha Perkasa dan Agung. Dan itulah agama yang lurus dan benar. [Surat al-Bayyinah (98): 5]. Dalil untuk puasa (siyam) adalah firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi: Artinya ibadah kepada Allah, menjadikan agama murni untuk-Nya, menjauhi syirik dan mendirikan shalat serta membayar zakat. Agama yang benar dan lurus, tidak mengandung kebengkokan karena ia adalah agama yang ditetapkan oleh Allah Yang Maha Perkasa dan Agung, dan agama Allah adalah lurus dan tegak, sebagaimana Allah Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman: 'Dan bahwa (yang Kami perintahkan) ini adalah jalan-Ku yang lurus, maka ikutilah dia; dan janganlah kamu mengikuti jalan-jalan (yang lain), karena jalan-jalan itu mencerai-beraikan kamu dari jalan-Nya.' [Surat al-An'am (6): 153]. Sebagaimana ayah yang mulia yang disebutkan oleh penulis mengandung penyebutan ibadah, dan shalat serta zakat, maka ia mencakup hakikat tauhid yaitu menjadikan ibadah murni untuk Allah Yang Maha Perkasa dan Agung, tanpa kecenderungan kepada syirik. Sehingga barangsiapa tidak menjadikan ibadahnya murni untuk Allah bukanlah seorang muwahhid (orang yang bertauhid), dan barangsiapa menjadikan ibadahnya untuk selain Allah bukanlah orang yang bertauhid. Dalil untuk kewajibannya adalah firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi: 'Hai orang-orang yang beriman, diwajibkan atas kamu berpuasa sebagaimana diwajibkan atas orang-orang sebelum kamu agar kamu bertakwa.' [Surat al-Baqarah (2): 183]. Firman-Nya 'sebagaimana diwajibkan atas orang-orang sebelum kamu' mengandung sejumlah pelajaran yang bermanfaat: (i) Pentingnya puasa karena Allah Yang Maha Perkasa dan Agung mewajibkan puasa atas umat-umat sebelum kita. Ini menunjukkan bahwa itu adalah sesuatu yang dicintai Allah Yang Maha Perkasa dan Agung, dan bahwa itu adalah kewajiban bagi setiap umat. (ii) Kemudahan bagi umat ini karena ia tidak sendirian dalam memiliki kewajiban berpuasa ini yang dibebankan kepadanya, suatu kewajiban yang mungkin sulit bagi jiwa dan tubuh. (iii) Indikasi bahwa Allah Yang Maha Tinggi menyempurnakan agama bagi umat ini dan melengkapinya dengan keutamaan-keutamaan yang diberikan kepada umat-umat sebelumnya. 'Hai orang-orang yang beriman, diwajibkan atas kamu berpuasa sebagaimana diwajibkan atas orang-orang sebelum kamu agar kamu bertakwa.' [Surat al-Baqarah (2): 183]. Dalam ayah ini Allah Yang Maha Perkasa dan Agung menjelaskan hikmah di balik puasa, dalam firman-Nya bahwa kamu harus bertakwa kepada Allah melalui puasa, dan mencapai karakteristik takwa yang dihasilkan darinya. Manfaat ini ditunjukkan oleh Nabi dalam sabdanya: 'Barangsiapa tidak meninggalkan ucapan dusta dan perbuatan dusta, maka Allah tidak butuh ia meninggalkan makanan dan minumannya.' [Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bukhari]. Dalil untuk haji adalah firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi: 'Mengerjakan haji adalah kewajiban manusia terhadap Allah, yaitu (bagi) orang yang sanggup mengadakan perjalanan ke Baitullah. Barangsiapa mengingkari (kewajiban haji), maka sesungguhnya Allah Maha Kaya (tidak memerlukan sesuatu) dari semesta alam.' [Surat Ali Imran (3): 97]. Dalil untuk kewajibannya adalah firman Allah Yang Maha Tinggi: 'Mengerjakan haji adalah kewajiban manusia terhadap Allah, yaitu (bagi) orang yang sanggup mengadakan perjalanan ke Baitullah. Barangsiapa mengingkari (kewajiban haji), maka sesungguhnya Allah Maha Kaya (tidak memerlukan sesuatu) dari semesta alam.' [Surat Ali Imran (3): 97]. Ayah ini diturunkan pada tahun kesembilan setelah hijrah, ketika haji menjadi kewajiban. Namun Allah Yang Maha Perkasa dan Agung berfirman: 'bagi orang yang sanggup.' Sehingga menunjukkan bahwa haji tidak wajib bagi orang yang tidak mampu melaksanakannya.
content_en: The evidence for the Prayer (as-salaat) and the zakaat, [112] and the explanation of tawhid is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High, "And they were not commanded except that they should worship Allaah alone, making their worship and obedience purely for Him, upon the true Religion and free from shirk; and that they should establish the Prayer, and pay the zakaat [113] – ____________________________________________________________ That is, the evidence that the Prayer and the zakaat are from the Religion, is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High, "And they were not commanded except that they should worship Allaah alone, making their worship and obedience purely for Him, upon the true Religion and free from shirk; and that they should establish the Prayer, and pay the zakaat." [surah al-Bayyinah (98):5] This Aayah is general and covers all types of worship, so a person must perform all of them purely and sincerely for Allaah, the Mighty and Majestic, being upon the straight and true Religion and following the Revealed way prescribed by Him. This is an example of something particular being mentioned after the general. So establishment of the Prayer and payment of the zakaat are types of worship; however Allaah, the one free of all imperfections, mentioned them specifically due to their great importance. So the Prayer is a bodily worship, and the zakaat is a worship involving giving wealth, and they are mentioned jointly in the Book of Allaah, the Mighty and Majestic. and that [114], the straight and true Religion" [115] [surah alBayyinah (98):5]. And the evidence for Fasting (siyaam) [116] is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High, ________________________________________________________________________ i.e. the worship of Allaah, making the Religion purely for Him, keeping aloof from shirk and establishment of the Prayer and payment of the zakaat. The true and straight Religion, containing no crookedness since it is the Religion laid down by Allaah, the Mighty and Majestic, and Allaah's Religion is straight and upright, just as Allaah, the Most High, says, "This is My Straight Path, so follow it and do not follow any of the other paths, for they will split you and take you away from that way which He prescribed for you." [surah al-An'aam (6):153] Just as the noble Aayah mentioned by the author contains a mention of worship, and the Prayer and the zakaat, then it comprises the reality of tawhid and that it is to make worship purely for Allaah, the Mighty and Majestic, without any inclination to shirk. So whoever does not make his worship purely for Allaah is not a muwahhid (person upon tawhid), and whoever makes his worship for other than Allaah is not a person upon tawhid. The proof for its obligation is the saying of Allaah, the Most High, "O you who believe Fasting is prescribed as an obligation for you as it was prescribed as an obligation for those who came before you." [surah alBaqarah (2): 183] His Saying “as it was prescribed as an obligation for those who came before you" contains a number of beneficial lessons: (i) The importance of Fasting since Allaah, the Mighty and Majestic, made Fasting obligatory upon the nations before us. This shows that it is something which Allaah, the Mighty and Majestic, loves, and that it was binding upon every nation. (ii) Ease for this ummah (nation) since it is not alone in having this duty to fast placed upon it, a duty which may be difficult for the souls and the bodies. (iii) An indication that Allaah, the Most High, perfected the Religion for this ummah and completed it with the virtues which were given to earlier nations. "O you who believe Fasting is prescribed as an obligation for you as it was prescribed as an obligation for those who came before you, so that you may attain taqwa (obedience to Allaah and avoidance of whatever He has forbidden)" [117] [surah alBaqarah (2): 183]. ___________________________________________________________________ In this Aayah Allaah, the Mighty and Majestic, makes clear the wisdom behind fasting, in His Saying that you should fear Allaah through fasting, and attain the characteristics of taqwa resulting from it. This benefit was indicated by the Prophet in his Saying, "Whoever does not abandon falsehood and evil actions, then Aliaah has no need that he should abandon his food and drink" [Reported by al-Bukhaaree (Eng. transl. 3/70/127)]. The evidence for hajj [118] is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High, "And hajj to Allaah's sacred House is an obligation upon those able to perform it; and whoever refuses and rejects the obligation of hajj to Allaah's House, then Allaah has no need of him or of any of the creation" [119] [surah Aal-'Imraan (3):97]. ________________________________________________________ The proof for its obligation is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High, "And hajj to Allaah's sacred House is an obligation to those able to perform it; and whoever refuses and rejects the obligation of hajj to Allaah's House, then Allaah has no need of him or of any of the creation," [surah Aal-'Imraan (3):97] This Aayah was sent down in the ninth year after the hijrah, when the hajj became an obligation. However Allaah, the Mighty and Majestic, says, “those able to perform it." So it shows that hajj is not an obligation upon one unable to perform it.
content_ar: The evidence for the الصلاة (as-salaat) and the zakaat, [112] and the explanation of tawhid is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High, "And they were not commanded except that they should worship Allaah alone, making their worship and obedience purely for Him, upon the true Religion and free from الشرك; and that they should establish the الصلاة, and pay the zakaat [113] – ____________________________________________________________ That is, the evidence that the الصلاة and the zakaat are from the Religion, is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High, "And they were not commanded except that they should worship Allaah alone, making their worship and obedience purely for Him, upon the true Religion and free from الشرك; and that they should establish the الصلاة, and pay the zakaat." [السورة al-Bayyinah (98):5] This Aayah is general and covers all types of worship, لذا a person must perform all of them purely and sincerely for Allaah, the Mighty and Majestic, being upon the straight and true Religion and following the Revealed الطريق prescribed by Him. This is an example of something particular being mentioned بعد the general. لذا establishment of the الصلاة and payment of the zakaat are types of worship; غير أن Allaah, the one free of all imperfections, mentioned them specifically بسبب their great importance. لذا the الصلاة is a bodily worship, and the zakaat is a worship involving giving الثروة, and they are mentioned jointly in the الكتاب of Allaah, the Mighty and Majestic. and that [114], the straight and true Religion" [115] [السورة alBayyinah (98):5]. And the evidence for الصيام (siyaam) [116] is the Saying of Allaah, the Most High, ________________________________________________________________________ i.e. the worship of Allaah, making the Religion purely for Him, keeping aloof from الشرك and establishment of the الصلاة and payment of the zakaat. The true and straight Religion, containing no crookedness بما أن it is the Religion laid down by Allaah, the Mighty and Majestic, and Allaah's Religion is straight and upright, just as Allaah, the Most High, says, "This is My Straight الطريق, لذا follow it and do not follow any of the other paths, for they will split you and take you away from that الطريق which He prescribed for you." [السورة al-An'aam (6):153] Just as the noble Aayah mentioned by the author contains a mention of worship, and the الصلاة and the zakaat, then it comprises the reality of tawhid and that it is to make worship purely for Allaah, the Mighty and Majestic, without any inclination to الشرك. لذا whoever does not make his worship purely for Allaah is not a muwahhid (person upon tawhid), and whoever makes his worship for other than Allaah is not a person upon tawhid. The proof for its الالتزام is the saying of Allaah, the Most High, "O you who believe الصيام is prescribed as an الالتزام for you as it was prescribed as an الالتزام for those who came قبل you." [السورة alBaqarah (2): 183] His Saying “as it was prescribed as an الالتزام f
